title: chisocheton macranthus
intro: Chisocheton macranthus, also known as the Bunga Kertas or Paper Flower tree, is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Chisocheton macranthus include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and skin care. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
